151	        if self.debug_mode:
152	            assert (
153	                need_size % self.page_size == 0
154	            ), "The allocation size should be page-aligned"
155	
156	        num_pages = need_size // self.page_size
157	        if self.need_sort and num_pages > len(self.free_pages):
158	            self.merge_and_sort_free()
159	        if num_pages > len(self.free_pages):
160	            return None
161	
162	        out_pages = self.free_pages[:num_pages]
163	        self.free_pages = self.free_pages[num_pages:]
164	
165	        out_indices = (
166	            out_pages[:, None] * self.page_size
167	            + torch.arange(self.page_size, device=self.device)
168	        ).reshape(-1)

170	        return out_indices

172	    def alloc_extend(
173	        self,
174	        prefix_lens: torch.Tensor,
175	        prefix_lens_cpu: torch.Tensor,
176	        seq_lens: torch.Tensor,
177	        seq_lens_cpu: torch.Tensor,
178	        last_loc: torch.Tensor,

181	    ):

184	                (last_loc + 1) % self.page_size == prefix_lens % self.page_size
185	            )
186	
187	        bs = len(prefix_lens)
188	        if self.need_sort and extend_num_tokens // self.page_size + bs + 1 > len(
189	            self.free_pages
190	        ):
191	            self.merge_and_sort_free()

193	        out_indices = torch.empty(
194	            (extend_num_tokens,), dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device
195	        )

204	            self.page_size,

209

219	        self.free_pages = self.free_pages[num_new_pages:]

231	            )
232	
233	        bs = len(seq_lens)

235	            self.merge_and_sort_free()

237	        out_indices = torch.empty((bs,), dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device)
238	        alloc_decode_kernel[(bs,)](
239	            seq_lens,
240	            last_loc,
241	            self.free_pages,
242	            out_indices,
243	            next_power_of_2(bs),
244	            self.page_size,
245	        )
246	
247	        if self.debug_mode:
248	            assert len(torch.unique(out_indices)) == len(out_indices)
249	
250	        num_new_pages = get_num_new_pages(

252	            page_size=self.page_size,
253	            decode=True,
254	        )
255	        if num_new_pages > len(self.free_pages):

261	    def free(self, free_index: torch.Tensor):
262	        if free_index.numel() == 0:
263	            return

273	
274	        if self.debug_mode:
275	            assert len(torch.unique(self.free_pages)) == len(self.free_pages)
276

279	        self.free_pages = torch.arange(
280	            1, self.num_pages + 1, dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device
281	        )

283	        self.free_group = []

"""
Copyright 2025 SGLang Team
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

"""
Page-aligned memory pool.
"""


from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

import torch

from sglang.kernels.ops.memory.allocator import (
    alloc_decode_kernel,
    alloc_extend_kernel,
)
from sglang.srt.mem_cache.allocator.base import BaseTokenToKVPoolAllocator
from sglang.srt.utils import (
    get_bool_env_var,
    get_num_new_pages,
    is_hip,
    next_power_of_2,
)

_is_hip = is_hip()

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from sglang.srt.mem_cache.memory_pool import KVCache


def alloc_extend_naive(
    prefix_lens,
    seq_lens,
    last_loc,
    free_pages,
    out_indices,
    page_size,
    device,
):
    extend_lens = seq_lens - prefix_lens
    end_pos = torch.cumsum(extend_lens, 0)
    start_pos = end_pos - extend_lens
    num_new_pages = (seq_lens + page_size - 1) // page_size - (
        prefix_lens + page_size - 1
    ) // page_size
    num_full_new_pages = (seq_lens) // page_size - (
        prefix_lens + page_size - 1
    ) // page_size
    need_page = num_new_pages - num_full_new_pages
    end_new_pages = torch.cumsum(num_new_pages, 0)
    start_new_pages = end_new_pages - num_new_pages
    pos_in_page = torch.arange(page_size, device=device, dtype=torch.int32)
    for i in range(len(prefix_lens)):
        num1 = (
            min(
                seq_lens[i],
                (prefix_lens[i] + page_size - 1) // page_size * page_size,
            )
            - prefix_lens[i]
        )
        if num1:
            out_indices[start_pos[i] : start_pos[i] + num1] = (
                last_loc[i] + 1 + pos_in_page[:num1].view(-1)
            )

        if prefix_lens[i] + num1 == seq_lens[i]:
            continue

        num2 = (
            seq_lens[i] // page_size - (prefix_lens[i] + page_size - 1) // page_size
        ) * page_size
        if num2:
            pages = (
                free_pages[start_new_pages[i] : end_new_pages[i] - need_page[i]]
                * page_size
            )
            out_indices[start_pos[i] + num1 : start_pos[i] + num1 + num2] = (
                pages.view(-1, 1) + pos_in_page.view(1, -1)
            ).view(-1)

        if prefix_lens[i] + num1 + num2 == seq_lens[i]:
            continue

        num3 = seq_lens[i] - seq_lens[i] // page_size * page_size
        if num3:
            out_indices[end_pos[i] - num3 : end_pos[i]] = (
                free_pages[end_new_pages[i] - 1] * page_size + pos_in_page[:num3]
            ).view(-1)


class PagedTokenToKVPoolAllocator(BaseTokenToKVPoolAllocator):
    """
    An allocator managing the indices to kv cache data.

    This class has the same interface as `TokenToKVPoolAllocator` but the output
    of one request is always page-aligned.

    TODO: fuse last_loc into the kernel.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        size: int,
        page_size: int,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
        device: str,
        kvcache: KVCache,
        need_sort: bool,
    ):
        super().__init__(size, page_size, dtype, device, kvcache, need_sort)
        self.num_pages = size // page_size
        self.debug_mode = get_bool_env_var("SGLANG_DEBUG_MEMORY_POOL")

        # Pre-warm the torch.unique HIP kernel used in free(). When a request
        # finishes with a prompt that already exists in the radix tree (e.g.
        # bench_serving sending the same warmup+measured prompt), the radix
        # cache's _insert_helper frees the duplicate KV indices via
        # token_to_kv_pool_allocator.free(value[start:prefix_len]). That call
        # path runs `torch.unique(free_index // self.page_size)` on a
        # ~prompt_len-sized int64 tensor. The first such call on AMD ROCm
        # JIT-compiles rocPRIM sort/unique kernels and costs ~200ms, which
        # shows up as a mysterious "second-request slow" (Run 1) for
        # repeated-prompt benchmarks. Running it once at init time moves
        # that JIT cost to startup. This is a ROCm-only JIT cost, so the
        # warm-up is gated on _is_hip and skipped on other platforms.
        if _is_hip and torch.cuda.is_available():
            try:
                _warmup = torch.arange(1024, dtype=torch.int64, device=device)
                _ = torch.unique(_warmup // page_size)
                torch.cuda.synchronize()
            except Exception:
                pass
        self.clear()

    def alloc(self, need_size: int):
        # page-aligned allocation, returning contiguous indices of pages
        if self.debug_mode:
            assert (
                need_size % self.page_size == 0
            ), "The allocation size should be page-aligned"

        num_pages = need_size // self.page_size
        if self.need_sort and num_pages > len(self.free_pages):
            self.merge_and_sort_free()
        if num_pages > len(self.free_pages):
            return None

        out_pages = self.free_pages[:num_pages]
        self.free_pages = self.free_pages[num_pages:]

        out_indices = (
            out_pages[:, None] * self.page_size
            + torch.arange(self.page_size, device=self.device)
        ).reshape(-1)

        return out_indices

    def alloc_extend(
        self,
        prefix_lens: torch.Tensor,
        prefix_lens_cpu: torch.Tensor,
        seq_lens: torch.Tensor,
        seq_lens_cpu: torch.Tensor,
        last_loc: torch.Tensor,
        extend_num_tokens: int,
        num_new_pages: int = None,
    ):
        if self.debug_mode:
            assert torch.all(
                (last_loc + 1) % self.page_size == prefix_lens % self.page_size
            )

        bs = len(prefix_lens)
        if self.need_sort and extend_num_tokens // self.page_size + bs + 1 > len(
            self.free_pages
        ):
            self.merge_and_sort_free()

        out_indices = torch.empty(
            (extend_num_tokens,), dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device
        )

        alloc_extend_kernel[(bs,)](
            prefix_lens,
            seq_lens,
            last_loc,
            self.free_pages,
            out_indices,
            next_power_of_2(bs),
            self.page_size,
        )

        if self.debug_mode:
            assert len(torch.unique(out_indices)) == len(out_indices)

        if num_new_pages is None:
            num_new_pages = get_num_new_pages(
                seq_lens=seq_lens_cpu,
                page_size=self.page_size,
                prefix_lens=prefix_lens_cpu,
            )
        if num_new_pages > len(self.free_pages):
            return None

        self.free_pages = self.free_pages[num_new_pages:]
        return out_indices

    def alloc_decode(
        self,
        seq_lens: torch.Tensor,
        seq_lens_cpu: torch.Tensor,
        last_loc: torch.Tensor,
    ):
        if self.debug_mode:
            assert torch.all(
                (last_loc + 2) % self.page_size == seq_lens % self.page_size
            )

        bs = len(seq_lens)
        if self.need_sort and bs > len(self.free_pages):
            self.merge_and_sort_free()

        out_indices = torch.empty((bs,), dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device)
        alloc_decode_kernel[(bs,)](
            seq_lens,
            last_loc,
            self.free_pages,
            out_indices,
            next_power_of_2(bs),
            self.page_size,
        )

        if self.debug_mode:
            assert len(torch.unique(out_indices)) == len(out_indices)

        num_new_pages = get_num_new_pages(
            seq_lens=seq_lens_cpu,
            page_size=self.page_size,
            decode=True,
        )
        if num_new_pages > len(self.free_pages):
            return None

        self.free_pages = self.free_pages[num_new_pages:]
        return out_indices

    def free(self, free_index: torch.Tensor):
        if free_index.numel() == 0:
            return

        if self.is_not_in_free_group:
            free_page_indices = torch.unique(free_index // self.page_size)
            if self.need_sort:
                self.release_pages = torch.cat((free_page_indices, self.release_pages))
            else:
                self.free_pages = torch.cat((free_page_indices, self.free_pages))
        else:
            self.free_group.append(free_index)

        if self.debug_mode:
            assert len(torch.unique(self.free_pages)) == len(self.free_pages)

    def clear(self):
        # The padded slot 0 is used for writing dummy outputs from padded tokens.
        self.free_pages = torch.arange(
            1, self.num_pages + 1, dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device
        )
        self.is_not_in_free_group = True
        self.free_group = []
        self.release_pages = torch.empty((0,), dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device)

    def get_cpu_copy(self, indices, mamba_indices=None):
        return self._kvcache.get_cpu_copy(indices, mamba_indices=mamba_indices)

    def load_cpu_copy(self, kv_cache_cpu, indices, mamba_indices=None):
        return self._kvcache.load_cpu_copy(
            kv_cache_cpu, indices, mamba_indices=mamba_indices
        )

14	"""MultiEndedAllocator: one allocator per sub-pool over a `UnifiedKVPool`.
15	
16	`alloc*` run the upstream kernels ONCE in virtual space using `free_virtual_ids`
17	as the free-page pointer, then bind consumed virtual pages to physical pages so
18	`translate_kv_loc` resolves. Public methods take/return TOKEN-granular tensors;
19	`free_virtual_ids` and the v2p/p2v tables are page-granular. For `page_size == 1`
20	page math collapses to slot math byte-identically.
21	"""
22	
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import inspect
26	import logging
27	import os
28	from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple
29	
30	import torch
31	from torch.profiler import record_function
32	
33	from sglang.kernels.ops.memory.virtual_slot import alloc_bind_inplace
34	from sglang.srt.environ import envs
35	from sglang.srt.mem_cache.allocator import BaseTokenToKVPoolAllocator
36	from sglang.srt.mem_cache.allocator.paged import (
37	    alloc_decode_kernel,
38	    alloc_extend_kernel,
39	)
40	from sglang.srt.mem_cache.allocator.swa import SWATokenToKVPoolAllocator
41	from sglang.srt.mem_cache.unified_memory_pool import UnifiedKVPool
42	from sglang.srt.utils.common import get_num_new_pages, next_power_of_2
43	
44	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
45	
46	
47	# OFF (default): cat unsorted, `_flush` sorts once. ON: sort after each cat.
48	_SORT_FREE_LIST_AFTER_MERGE = envs.SGLANG_SORT_FREE_LIST_AFTER_MERGE.get()
49	
50	
51	import atexit
52	import signal
53	import time as _time_mod  # local alias so tests can patch
54	import weakref
55	
56	_LAZY_COMPACTION_STATS_ENABLED = envs.SGLANG_LOG_LAZY_COMPACTION_STATS.get()
57	_LAZY_COMPACTION_STATS_INTERVAL_SEC = float(
58	    envs.SGLANG_LOG_LAZY_COMPACTION_STATS_INTERVAL_SEC.get()
59	)
60	# Signal handler emits each instance's final counters (atexit misses signal exits).
61	_STATS_INSTANCES: weakref.WeakSet[MultiEndedAllocator] = weakref.WeakSet()
62	_SIGNAL_HANDLERS_INSTALLED = False
63	
64	
65	def _emit_all_final_stats(reason: str) -> None:
66	    for inst in list(_STATS_INSTANCES):
67	        try:
68	            inst._emit_stats_final(reason=reason)
69	        except Exception:
70	            pass
71	
72	
73	def _signal_handler(signum, frame):
74	    try:
75	        sig_name = signal.Signals(signum).name
76	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
77	        sig_name = str(signum)
78	    _emit_all_final_stats(reason=sig_name)
79	    signal.signal(signum, signal.SIG_DFL)
80	    os.kill(os.getpid(), signum)
81	
82	
83	def _install_signal_handlers_once() -> None:
84	    global _SIGNAL_HANDLERS_INSTALLED
85	    if _SIGNAL_HANDLERS_INSTALLED:
86	        return
87	    _SIGNAL_HANDLERS_INSTALLED = True
88	    # Only override the default handler (the scheduler subprocess installs none).
89	    for sig in (signal.SIGTERM, signal.SIGINT):
90	        try:
91	            prev = signal.getsignal(sig)
92	            if prev in (signal.SIG_DFL, signal.SIG_IGN, None):
93	                signal.signal(sig, _signal_handler)
94	        except (ValueError, OSError):
95	            # Raises off the main thread — skip.
96	            pass
97	
98	
99	class MultiEndedAllocator(BaseTokenToKVPoolAllocator):
100	    """Allocator for one sub-pool over a `UnifiedKVPool`."""
101	
102	    def __init__(
103	        self,
104	        *,
105	        kvcache,
106	        unified_buffer: UnifiedKVPool,
107	        sub_pool_name: str,
108	        device: str,
109	        is_id_owner: bool,
110	        page_size: int = 1,
111	        need_sort: bool = False,
112	        forward_stream: Optional[torch.cuda.Stream] = None,
113	        lazy_compaction: bool = False,
114	    ):
115	        spec = unified_buffer.spec(sub_pool_name)
116	        max_slots = unified_buffer.max_slots(sub_pool_name)
117	        super().__init__(
118	            size=max_slots,
119	            page_size=page_size,
120	            dtype=spec.get_dtype(),
121	            device=device,
122	            kvcache=kvcache,
123	            need_sort=need_sort,
124	        )
125	        self.unified_buffer = unified_buffer
126	        self.sub_pool_name = sub_pool_name
127	        self.spec = spec
128	        self.max_slots = max_slots
129	        self.grow_direction = spec.grow_direction
130	        self.entry_bytes = spec.entry_bytes()
131	        self.min_slot_index = unified_buffer.min_slot_index(sub_pool_name)
132	        self.is_id_owner = is_id_owner
133	        # Overlap mode: `free` drops a wait_stream(forward_stream) barrier so its
134	        # v2p writes + move kernel serialize after the in-flight forward.
135	        self.forward_stream = forward_stream
136	
137	        # --- Page-aware bookkeeping ---
138	        # `min_page_index` = ceil(min_slot_index / page_size), keeping the
139	        # reserved-sink invariant (min_page_index * entry_bytes_per_page >= entry_max).
140	        self.page_size = page_size
141	        self.num_pages = max_slots // page_size
142	        self.min_page_index = (self.min_slot_index + page_size - 1) // page_size
143	        self.entry_bytes_per_page = self.entry_bytes * page_size
144	
145	        # v2p / p2v sized by PAGES. Page 0 is the padding anchor; trailing row is
146	        # the -1 sentinel.
147	        self.virtual_to_physical = torch.full(
148	            (self.num_pages + 1,),
149	            -1,
150	            dtype=torch.int64,
151	            device=device,
152	        )
153	        self.physical_to_virtual = torch.full(
154	            (self.num_pages + 1,),
155	            -1,
156	            dtype=torch.int64,
157	            device=device,
158	        )
159	        # Back-compat alias (count of virtual PAGES) consulted by is_slot_allocated.
160	        self.num_virtual_ids = self.num_pages
161	
162	        self._peer: Optional[MultiEndedAllocator] = None
163	
164	        # Inverse history of relocations (spec rollback), at PAGE granularity.
165	        self._inverse_history: List[Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]] = (
166	            []
167	        )
168	
169	        # --- Lazy compaction state (all unused when lazy_compaction=False) ---
170	        # `_free_phys_pages`: GPU free list of physical PAGE ids, sorted at `_flush`.
171	        # `_pending_reuse`: compaction-src pages whose remap completed but whose
172	        #   reader event hasn't fired — can't re-enter the free list until the read
173	        #   settles (else a future alloc's WRITE races the READ).
174	        # `live_page_count`: CPU slot-conservation counter, invariant under compaction.
175	        # KV copy and v2p/p2v remap both run on `schedule_stream`, so single-stream
176	        # ordering serializes them — no separate copy-done event needed.
177	        self.lazy_compaction = lazy_compaction
178	        self._free_phys_pages: torch.Tensor = torch.empty(
179	            0, dtype=torch.int64, device=device
180	        )
181	        # Keyed by Event, ONE entry per BATCH. `(cpu_list, gpu_tensor)`: cpu_list
182	        # drives the Set update (no sync); gpu_tensor is the SAME tensor
183	        # `_commit_move_batch` remapped, kept alive so drain cats it without an H2D.
184	        self._pending_reuse: Dict[
185	            torch.cuda.Event,
186	            Tuple[List[int], torch.Tensor],
187	        ] = {}
188	        # CPU mirror of `_pending_reuse` for O(1) membership in the survivor walk.
189	        self._pending_reuse_pages_cpu: Set[int] = set()
190	        # Cumulative observability counters (NOT reset at clear()).
191	        self._stats_n_free_lazy: int = 0
192	        self._stats_n_release_batch: int = 0
193	        self._stats_n_drain_calls: int = 0
194	        self._stats_n_drain_did_work: int = 0
195	        self._stats_n_drained_pages_total: int = 0
196	        self._stats_n_flush_calls: int = 0
197	        self._stats_n_flush_did_work: int = 0
198	        self._stats_n_flush_moves: int = 0
199	        self._stats_n_pages_absorbed: int = 0
200	        self._stats_peak_free_list_len: int = 0
201	        self._stats_peak_pending_pages: int = 0
202	        self._stats_n_emits: int = 0
203	        self._stats_last_emit_ts: float = _time_mod.monotonic()
204	        self._stats_final_emitted: bool = False
205	        if _LAZY_COMPACTION_STATS_ENABLED:
206	            atexit.register(self._emit_stats_final, reason="atexit")
207	            _STATS_INSTANCES.add(self)
208	            _install_signal_handlers_once()
209	        self.live_page_count = 0
210	        self._latest_forward_done_event: Optional[torch.cuda.Event] = None
211	        # Most-recent forward's (done_event, out_cache_loc_virtual) for `_flush`'s
212	        # write-race check. Single slot: at most ONE forward in flight per call site.
213	        # Only the tensor reference is stored; `_flush` materializes the write-set
214	        # lazily, avoiding a launch-time sync.
215	        self._inflight_forward: Optional[Tuple[torch.cuda.Event, torch.Tensor]] = None
216	
217	        # Per-call move cap on NON-urgent `_flush`: bounds work per `on_idle()` so a
218	        # large backlog doesn't block ZMQ IPC; the next flush picks up the rest.
219	        # Urgent (alloc-shortfall retry) is uncapped — must drain everything.
220	        self._lazy_max_moves_per_call = int(
221	            os.environ.get("SGLANG_LAZY_COMPACTION_MAX_MOVES_PER_CALL", "4096")
222	        )
223	
224	        self.clear()
225	
226	        logger.info(
227	            "[unified-memory-pool] MultiEndedAllocator(%r) ready: grow=%s, max_slots=%d, "
228	            "min_slot_index=%d, page_size=%d, num_pages=%d, min_page_index=%d, "
229	            "entry_bytes=%d, entry_bytes_per_page=%d, is_id_owner=%s, "
230	            "initial_watermark_page=%d, allocatable_pages=%d",
231	            self.sub_pool_name,
232	            self.grow_direction,
233	            self.max_slots,
234	            self.min_slot_index,
235	            self.page_size,
236	            self.num_pages,
237	            self.min_page_index,
238	            self.entry_bytes,
239	            self.entry_bytes_per_page,
240	            self.is_id_owner,
241	            self.watermark_physical,
242	            self.num_pages - self.min_page_index,
243	        )
244	
245	    # -- peer binding --
246	
247	    def bind_peer(self, peer: MultiEndedAllocator) -> None:
248	        self._peer = peer
249	
250	    @property
251	    def peer(self) -> Optional[MultiEndedAllocator]:
252	        return self._peer
253	
254	    # -- state --
255	
256	    def clear(self) -> None:
257	        """Reset to initial state. Pages in `[0, min_page_index)` are reserved."""
258	        if self.grow_direction == "up":
259	            self.watermark_physical = self.min_page_index
260	        else:
261	            self.watermark_physical = self.num_pages - 1
262	        self.virtual_to_physical.fill_(-1)
263	        # Virtual page 0 <-> physical page 0 (padding sink).
264	        self.virtual_to_physical[0] = 0
265	        self.virtual_to_physical[-1] = -1  # trailing sentinel
266	        self.physical_to_virtual.fill_(-1)
267	        self.physical_to_virtual[0] = 0
268	        self.physical_to_virtual[-1] = -1
269	        if self.is_id_owner:
270	            self.free_virtual_ids = torch.arange(
271	                self.min_page_index,
272	                self.num_pages,
273	                dtype=torch.int64,
274	                device=self.device,
275	            )
276	        else:
277	            self.free_virtual_ids = None
278	        self.is_not_in_free_group = True
279	        self.free_group: List[torch.Tensor] = []
280	        self._inverse_history.clear()
281	        self._free_phys_pages = torch.empty(0, dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device)
282	        self._pending_reuse.clear()
283	        self._pending_reuse_pages_cpu.clear()
284	        self.live_page_count = 0
285	        self._inflight_forward = None
286	        self._latest_forward_done_event = None
287	
288	    def backup_state(self):
289	        # Spec-decode allocates only inside a backup window (no free), so
290	        # `_inverse_history` doesn't grow under correct usage.
291	        return (
292	            self.watermark_physical,
293	            (len(self.free_virtual_ids) if self.is_id_owner else None),
294	            len(self._inverse_history),
295	        )
296	
297	    def restore_state(self, state):
298	        watermark, n_free_virtual, n_inverse = state
299	        self.watermark_physical = watermark
300	        if self.is_id_owner and n_free_virtual is not None:
301	            pass  # spec asserted off; no free-list rollback.
302	        new_entries = self._inverse_history[n_inverse:]
303	        if new_entries:
304	            logger.warning(
305	                "MultiEndedAllocator.restore_state: %d relocation(s) recorded inside "
306	                "a backup window (sub_pool=%s). Eager compaction is not fully "
307	                "reversible; SGLang's spec path should not produce a free() inside a "
308	                "backup window.",
309	                len(new_entries),
310	                self.sub_pool_name,
311	            )
312	        del self._inverse_history[n_inverse:]
313	        return new_entries
314	
315	    def clear_inverse_history(self) -> None:
316	        self._inverse_history.clear()
317	
318	    # -- size reporting --
319	
320	    def _allocated_pages(self) -> int:
321	        """Number of allocated PAGES (TOKEN callers use `allocated_count()`)."""
322	        if self.grow_direction == "up":
323	            return max(0, self.watermark_physical - self.min_page_index)
324	        return max(0, self.num_pages - 1 - self.watermark_physical)
325	
326	    def allocated_count(self) -> int:
327	        """LIVE allocated TOKENS (excludes lazy holes / pending).
328	
329	        TOKENS, not pages — the leak checker's invariant is in tokens. Lazy mode
330	        uses `live_page_count` (invariant under compaction); the watermark span
331	        over-counts because holes/pending sit inside it but aren't live.
332	        """
333	        if self.lazy_compaction:
334	            return self.live_page_count * self.page_size
335	        return self._allocated_pages() * self.page_size
336	
337	    def is_slot_allocated(self, slot: int) -> bool:
338	        """Whether the PAGE containing this virtual id is in use."""
339	        virt_page = slot // self.page_size
340	        if virt_page < 0 or virt_page >= self.num_pages:
341	            return False
342	        return int(self.virtual_to_physical[virt_page].item()) != -1
343	
344	    def allocator_state_str(self) -> str:
345	        return (
346	            f"sub_pool={self.sub_pool_name!r}, grow_direction={self.grow_direction}, "
347	            f"is_id_owner={self.is_id_owner}, page_size={self.page_size}, "
348	            f"min_page_index={self.min_page_index}, "
349	            f"num_pages={self.num_pages}, "
350	            f"watermark_physical={self.watermark_physical}, "
351	            f"allocated_pages={self._allocated_pages()}"
352	        )
353	
354	    def _byte_high_frontier(self) -> int:
355	        """Byte just past this side's last-allocated page (grow-up) / buffer top (grow-down)."""
356	        if self.grow_direction == "up":
357	            return self.watermark_physical * self.entry_bytes_per_page
358	        return self.num_pages * self.entry_bytes_per_page
359	
360	    def _byte_low_frontier(self) -> int:
361	        """Byte starting this side's allocatable range (grow-up) / just below its lowest live page (grow-down)."""
362	        if self.grow_direction == "up":
363	            return self.min_page_index * self.entry_bytes_per_page
364	        return (self.watermark_physical + 1) * self.entry_bytes_per_page
365	
366	    def _current_gap_bytes(self) -> int:
367	        """Free byte band between this side's frontier and the peer's CURRENT frontier."""
368	        if self.grow_direction == "up":
369	            my_high = self._byte_high_frontier()
370	            peer_low = (
371	                self._peer._byte_low_frontier()
372	                if self._peer is not None
373	                else self.unified_buffer.total_bytes
374	            )
375	            return max(0, peer_low - my_high)
376	        my_low = self._byte_low_frontier()
377	        peer_high = self._peer._byte_high_frontier() if self._peer is not None else 0
378	        return max(0, my_low - peer_high)
379	
380	    def _available_tokens(self, extra_gap_bytes: int = 0) -> int:
381	        """Tokens allocatable given `extra_gap_bytes` of ADDED gap room
382	        (0 == current realizable; >0 == post-peer-compaction).
383	
384	        `pages_by_index_space` is OWN index headroom, unaffected by
385	        `extra_gap_bytes`: peer bytes can't add page indices to our own table.
386	        """
387	        gap_bytes = self._current_gap_bytes() + extra_gap_bytes
388	        pages_by_bytes = gap_bytes // self.entry_bytes_per_page
389	        pages_by_index_space = (
390	            self.num_pages - self.min_page_index - self._allocated_pages()
391	        )
392	        pages_extend = min(pages_by_bytes, pages_by_index_space)
393	        # Lazy: drainable holes don't consume new bytes.
394	        pages_drain = len(self._free_phys_pages) if self.lazy_compaction else 0
395	        return (pages_extend + pages_drain) * self.page_size
396	
397	    def available_size(self) -> int:
398	        """Tokens allocatable RIGHT NOW (no peer compaction).
399	
400	        Alloc shortfall gates consult this to decide whether to peer-flush, so it
401	        MUST NOT fold in peer holes (use `schedulable_available_size()` for that).
402	        """
403	        return self._available_tokens()
404	
405	    def _peer_drainable_hole_bytes(self) -> int:
406	        """Gap bytes a peer urgent flush would release. Only `_free_phys_pages`
407	        count — NOT `_pending_reuse` (awaiting an event) — so the credit is realizable.
408	        """
409	        peer = self._peer
410	        if peer is None or not peer.lazy_compaction:
411	            return 0
412	        return len(peer._free_phys_pages) * peer.entry_bytes_per_page
413	
414	    def schedulable_available_size(self) -> int:
415	        """Tokens allocatable AFTER a peer urgent-flush (realizable-with-compaction).
416	        Used by composite views; alloc gates use `available_size()`.
417	        """
418	        return self._available_tokens(extra_gap_bytes=self._peer_drainable_hole_bytes())
419	
420	    def _flush_peer_for_alloc(self, need_tokens: int) -> bool:
421	        """One urgent peer-flush on alloc shortfall; returns whether THIS side now
422	        has enough. Only PEER compaction releases gap bytes (own compaction is net 0).
423	        """
424	        if not (self.lazy_compaction and self._peer is not None):
425	            return False
426	        self._peer._flush(urgent=True)
427	        return need_tokens <= self.available_size()
428	
429	    # -- physical-slot / physical-page primitives --
430	
431	    def take_physical(self, need_size: int) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
432	        """Reserve `need_size` TOKENS (multiple of page_size), returning backing
433	        physical PAGE ids, or `None` on shortfall.
434	
435	        Eager: pure watermark advance. Lazy: drain `_free_phys_pages` holes first,
436	        then extend the watermark (extend first so state is untouched on failure).
437	        """
438	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.take_physical"):
439	            if need_size <= 0:
440	                return torch.empty(0, dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device)
441	            assert need_size % self.page_size == 0, (
442	                f"take_physical: need_size={need_size} must be a multiple of "
443	                f"page_size={self.page_size}"
444	            )
445	            num_pages = need_size // self.page_size
446	
447	            if not self.lazy_compaction:
448	                return self._take_physical_eager(num_pages)
449	
450	            # Lazy: slice the GPU free list (no D2H). sort ON: take deepest-in-band
451	            # per direction (greedy clustering). sort OFF: take from front.
452	            n_drain = min(num_pages, int(self._free_phys_pages.shape[0]))
453	            need_more = num_pages - n_drain
454	
455	            # Extend first (state untouched on failure), then drain holes.
456	            if need_more > 0:
457	                if not self._extend_watermark(need_more):
458	                    return None
459	
460	            if n_drain > 0:
461	                if _SORT_FREE_LIST_AFTER_MERGE:
462	                    if self.grow_direction == "up":
463	                        drained_t = self._free_phys_pages[:n_drain]
464	                        self._free_phys_pages = self._free_phys_pages[n_drain:]
465	                    else:
466	                        drained_t = self._free_phys_pages[-n_drain:].flip(0)
467	                        self._free_phys_pages = self._free_phys_pages[:-n_drain]
468	                else:
469	                    drained_t = self._free_phys_pages[:n_drain]
470	                    self._free_phys_pages = self._free_phys_pages[n_drain:]
471	            else:
472	                drained_t = None
473	
474	            self.live_page_count += num_pages
475	
476	            if drained_t is None:
477	                return self._take_physical_arange(num_pages)
478	
479	            # Pure drain — clone off the free-list view so rebindings don't pin it.
480	            if need_more == 0:
481	                return drained_t.clone()
482	
483	            # Mixed: drained holes ++ extended pages (`bind` is order-agnostic).
484	            if self.grow_direction == "up":
485	                new_wm = self.watermark_physical
486	                extended_t = torch.arange(
487	                    new_wm - need_more,
488	                    new_wm,
489	                    dtype=torch.int64,
490	                    device=self.device,
491	                )
492	            else:
493	                new_wm = self.watermark_physical
494	                extended_t = torch.arange(
495	                    new_wm + need_more,
496	                    new_wm,
497	                    -1,
498	                    dtype=torch.int64,
499	                    device=self.device,
500	                )
501	            return torch.cat([drained_t, extended_t])
502	
503	    def _take_physical_eager(self, num_pages: int) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
504	        """Eager-mode take_physical — contiguous range."""
505	        if self.grow_direction == "up":
506	            start = self.watermark_physical
507	            end_exclusive = start + num_pages
508	            if end_exclusive > self.num_pages:
509	                return None
510	            phys_pages = torch.arange(
511	                start, end_exclusive, dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device
512	            )
513	            self.watermark_physical = end_exclusive
514	            return phys_pages
515	        else:
516	            end = self.watermark_physical
517	            start = end - num_pages + 1
518	            if start < self.min_page_index:
519	                return None
520	            phys_pages = torch.arange(
521	                start, end + 1, dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device
522	            )
523	            self.watermark_physical -= num_pages
524	            return phys_pages
525	
526	    def _extend_watermark(self, num_pages: int) -> bool:
527	        """Advance the watermark by `num_pages` (lazy-path helper). Returns False
528	        on index-space overflow OR crossing the PEER's byte frontier.
529	        """
530	        if self.grow_direction == "up":
531	            new_wm = self.watermark_physical + num_pages
532	            if new_wm > self.num_pages:
533	                return False
534	            # Peer (grow-down) sits ABOVE; don't extend past its low frontier.
535	            if self._peer is not None:
536	                peer_low_pages = (
537	                    self._peer._byte_low_frontier() // self.entry_bytes_per_page
538	                )
539	                if new_wm > peer_low_pages:
540	                    return False
541	            self.watermark_physical = new_wm
542	        else:
543	            new_wm = self.watermark_physical - num_pages
544	            if new_wm < self.min_page_index - 1:
545	                return False
546	            # Peer (grow-up) sits BELOW; `new_wm + 1` (our new lowest live page)
547	            # must stay strictly above the peer's high frontier.
548	            if self._peer is not None:
549	                peer_high_pages = (
550	                    self._peer._byte_high_frontier() // self.entry_bytes_per_page
551	                )
552	                if new_wm + 1 < peer_high_pages:
553	                    return False
554	            self.watermark_physical = new_wm
555	        return True
556	
557	    def _take_physical_arange(self, num_pages: int) -> torch.Tensor:
558	        """Contiguous arange for an already-applied watermark extension."""
559	        if self.grow_direction == "up":
560	            return torch.arange(
561	                self.watermark_physical - num_pages,
562	                self.watermark_physical,
563	                dtype=torch.int64,
564	                device=self.device,
565	            )
566	        return torch.arange(
567	            self.watermark_physical + 1,
568	            self.watermark_physical + num_pages + 1,
569	            dtype=torch.int64,
570	            device=self.device,
571	        )
572	
573	    def take_physical_pages(self, num_pages: int) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
574	        """Page-granular wrapper around ``take_physical``."""
575	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.take_physical_pages"):
576	            return self.take_physical(num_pages * self.page_size)
577	
578	    def bind(self, virtual_ids: torch.Tensor, physical_ids: torch.Tensor) -> None:
579	        """Bind page-granular virtual ids to physical ids."""
580	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.bind"):
581	            self.virtual_to_physical[virtual_ids] = physical_ids
582	            self.physical_to_virtual[physical_ids] = virtual_ids
583	
584	    def bind_pages(
585	        self, virtual_pages: torch.Tensor, physical_pages: torch.Tensor
586	    ) -> None:
587	        """Page-granular alias of ``bind``."""
588	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.bind_pages"):
589	            self.bind(virtual_pages, physical_pages)
590	
591	    # -- fused take_physical_pages + bind_pages --
592	
593	    def _alloc_bind_fast_or_slow(
594	        self, v_pages: torch.Tensor, N: int
595	    ) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
596	        """Fuse `take_physical_pages` + `bind` into ONE Triton kernel when no
597	        holes need draining; fall through to the slow path (drains holes first)
598	        when holes exist. Returns physical page ids [N], or None on shortfall.
599	        """
600	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc._alloc_bind_fast_or_slow"):
601	            if N == 0:
602	                return torch.empty(0, dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device)
603	
604	            # FAST PATH: eager, or lazy with no current holes.
605	            if not self.lazy_compaction or self._free_phys_pages.numel() == 0:
606	                start_wm = self.watermark_physical  # kernel's `start_phys`
607	
608	                # Lazy uses `_extend_watermark` (index + peer checks); eager
609	                # inlines the index-only check to match `_take_physical_eager`.
610	                if self.lazy_compaction:
611	                    if not self._extend_watermark(N):
612	                        return None
613	                else:
614	                    if self.grow_direction == "up":
615	                        new_wm = start_wm + N
616	                        if new_wm > self.num_pages:
617	                            return None
618	                        self.watermark_physical = new_wm
619	                    else:
620	                        new_wm = start_wm - N
621	                        if new_wm < self.min_page_index - 1:
622	                            return None
623	                        self.watermark_physical = new_wm
624	
625	                # Lowest physical id of the new range (both directions yield
626	                # ascending `[start_phys, start_phys + N)`).
627	                if self.grow_direction == "up":
628	                    start_phys = start_wm
629	                else:
630	                    start_phys = start_wm - N + 1
631	
632	                phys_pages = alloc_bind_inplace(
633	                    v_pages,
634	                    self.virtual_to_physical,
635	                    self.physical_to_virtual,
636	                    start_phys,
637	                )
638	
639	                if self.lazy_compaction:  # live_page_count tracked only in lazy mode
640	                    self.live_page_count += N
641	                return phys_pages
642	
643	            # SLOW PATH: holes exist — drain them first, then bind.
644	            phys_pages = self.take_physical_pages(N)
645	            if phys_pages is None:
646	                return None
647	            self.bind(v_pages, phys_pages)
648	            return phys_pages
649	
650	    # -- translate (virtual TOKEN ids -> physical TOKEN ids) --
651	
652	    def translate_kv_loc(
653	        self,
654	        virt_tokens: torch.Tensor,
655	        *,
656	        out: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
657	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
658	        """Translate token-granular virtual ids to physical ids.
659	
660	        ``out=`` writes in-place into a caller-owned buffer — required under
661	        cuda-graph capture for buffer-stability (the captured graph records the
662	        gather against a fixed ``data_ptr``).
663	        """
664	        if out is not None:
665	            assert out.dtype == torch.int64, (
666	                f"translate_kv_loc: out= dtype must be int64 (matches v2p), "
667	                f"got {out.dtype}"
668	            )
669	            assert out.shape == virt_tokens.shape, (
670	                f"translate_kv_loc: out= shape {tuple(out.shape)} must match "
671	                f"virt_tokens shape {tuple(virt_tokens.shape)}"
672	            )
673	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.translate_kv_loc"):
674	            return self._translate_kv_loc_impl(virt_tokens, out)
675	
676	    def _translate_kv_loc_impl(
677	        self,
678	        virt_tokens: torch.Tensor,
679	        out: Optional[torch.Tensor],
680	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
681	        # Tombstone-safety clamp: tombstoned v2p entries (-1) must not reach
682	        # `k_buffer[-1]` (illegal access under captured graph replay). Clamp to 0
683	        # routes any tombstoned read/write to physical slot 0 — reserved
684	        # padding-sink space by the `min_slot_index` invariant (bytes [0, entry_max)
685	        # across all sub-pools hold no real data).
686	        if self.page_size == 1:
687	            if out is not None:
688	                # `index_select(out=out)` forbids index/out aliasing, but the
689	                # canonical caller does in-place `translate(kv_indices, out=kv_indices)`.
690	                # Route through a transient gather + `copy_` to satisfy that contract.
691	                tmp = torch.index_select(self.virtual_to_physical, 0, virt_tokens)
692	                tmp = torch.clamp_min(tmp, 0)
693	                out.copy_(tmp)
694	                return out
695	            result = torch.index_select(self.virtual_to_physical, 0, virt_tokens)
696	            return torch.clamp_min(result, 0)
697	        # page_size > 1: page math. `virt_pages`/`offsets` are fresh, so they
698	        # cannot alias `out` — `index_select(out=out)` is safe.
699	        virt_pages = virt_tokens // self.page_size
700	        offsets = virt_tokens % self.page_size
701	        if out is not None:
702	            torch.index_select(self.virtual_to_physical, 0, virt_pages, out=out)
703	            out.mul_(self.page_size)
704	            out.add_(offsets)
705	            out.clamp_(min=0)  # tombstoned page: -1*ps + offset in [-ps, -1]
706	            return out
707	        phys_pages = self.virtual_to_physical[virt_pages]
708	        result = phys_pages * self.page_size + offsets
709	        return torch.clamp_min(result, 0)
710	
711	    # -- alloc --
712	
713	    def alloc(self, need_size: int) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
714	        """Allocate `need_size` virtual TOKEN ids (id-owner only). Returns
715	        token-granular, page-structured ids, or None on shortfall.
716	
717	        `need_size` MUST be a multiple of `page_size`. All allocator GPU ops run
718	        on `schedule_stream`; `alloc` needs no `wait_stream` barrier because its
719	        v2p/p2v writes are picked up by the forward via the existing
720	        `forward_stream.wait_stream(schedule_stream)` at the top of `run_batch`.
721	        """
722	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.alloc"):
723	            assert self.is_id_owner, (
724	                f"MultiEndedAllocator({self.sub_pool_name!r}).alloc called on a "
725	                "non-id-owner allocator; use alloc_with_virtual instead"
726	            )
727	            if need_size <= 0:
728	                return torch.empty(0, dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device)
729	            assert need_size % self.page_size == 0, (
730	                f"MultiEndedAllocator({self.sub_pool_name!r}).alloc: need_size="
731	                f"{need_size} must be a multiple of page_size={self.page_size}"
732	            )
733	            if need_size > self.available_size():
734	                # Shortfall: flush the PEER, not own. Own compaction is net 0
735	                # (each move trades 1 hole for +1 gap byte); only peer compaction
736	                # releases bytes into the shared gap that own extension consumes.
737	                if not self._flush_peer_for_alloc(need_size):
738	                    return None
739	            num_pages = need_size // self.page_size
740	            v_pages = self.free_virtual_ids[:num_pages]
741	            self.free_virtual_ids = self.free_virtual_ids[num_pages:]
742	            phys_pages = self._alloc_bind_fast_or_slow(v_pages, num_pages)
743	            if phys_pages is None:
744	                self.free_virtual_ids = torch.cat([v_pages, self.free_virtual_ids])
745	                return None
746	            if self.page_size == 1:
747	                return v_pages  # v_pages already IS the token id list
748	            # Expand page ids to token ids: (P, 1) * S + (S,) → (P, S) → (P*S,).
749	            return (
750	                v_pages[:, None] * self.page_size
751	                + torch.arange(self.page_size, device=self.device)
752	            ).reshape(-1)
753	
754	    def alloc_with_virtual(self, virtual_pages: torch.Tensor) -> None:
755	        """Take physical PAGES for caller-supplied virtual PAGE ids
756	        (physical-holding non-owner; the SWA `swa` sub-allocator).
757	
758	        Input is virtual PAGE ids (not token ids): the composite snapshots the
759	        virtual pages before the id-owner consumes them from its free-list.
760	        """
761	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.alloc_with_virtual"):
762	            if virtual_pages.numel() == 0:
763	                return
764	            phys_pages = self._alloc_bind_fast_or_slow(
765	                virtual_pages, int(virtual_pages.numel())
766	            )
767	            assert phys_pages is not None, (
768	                f"MultiEndedAllocator({self.sub_pool_name!r}).alloc_with_virtual: out of "
769	                "physical room (the composite's byte-budget check should have caught this)"
770	            )
771	
772	    # -- paged alloc surface --
773	
774	    def alloc_extend(
775	        self,
776	        prefix_lens: torch.Tensor,
777	        prefix_lens_cpu: torch.Tensor,
778	        seq_lens: torch.Tensor,
779	        seq_lens_cpu: torch.Tensor,
780	        last_loc: torch.Tensor,
781	        extend_num_tokens: int,
782	        num_new_pages: Optional[int] = None,
783	    ) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
784	        """Allocate ``extend_num_tokens`` new tokens across ``bs`` requests,
785	        preserving the tail-page-reuse contract.
786	
787	        Runs the kernel in VIRTUAL space (``free_page_ptr == free_virtual_ids``),
788	        so ``out_indices`` are virtual token ids. Each consumed virtual page is
789	        then bound to a physical page on THIS sub-allocator; without that binding
790	        v2p stays -1 and translation yields negative ids → CUDA OOB.
791	        """
792	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.alloc_extend"):
793	            assert (
794	                self.is_id_owner
795	            ), f"alloc_extend on a non-id-owner allocator ({self.sub_pool_name!r})"
796	            if num_new_pages is None:
797	                num_new_pages = get_num_new_pages(
798	                    seq_lens=seq_lens_cpu,
799	                    page_size=self.page_size,
800	                    prefix_lens=prefix_lens_cpu,
801	                )
802	            if num_new_pages > len(self.free_virtual_ids):
803	                return None
804	            # Lazy: physical-capacity pre-check; on shortfall flush the PEER (own
805	            # compaction is internal — see `alloc`).
806	            need_tokens = num_new_pages * self.page_size
807	            if need_tokens > self.available_size():
808	                if not self._flush_peer_for_alloc(need_tokens):
809	                    return None
810	            bs = len(prefix_lens)
811	            if self.need_sort and extend_num_tokens // self.page_size + bs + 1 > len(
812	                self.free_virtual_ids
813	            ):
814	                self.merge_and_sort_free()
815	
816	            # Snapshot the virtual pages the kernel will consume, to bind them to
817	            # physical pages afterward (else v2p stays -1 → CUDA OOB).
818	            if num_new_pages > 0:
819	                new_virtual_pages = self.free_virtual_ids[:num_new_pages].clone()
820	            else:
821	                new_virtual_pages = None
822	
823	            out_indices = torch.empty(
824	                (extend_num_tokens,), dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device
825	            )
826	            # `free_virtual_ids` passed as `free_page_ptr`: the kernel does
827	            # `page_id * page_size + offset` regardless of virtual vs physical.
828	            with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.alloc_extend.kernel"):
829	                alloc_extend_kernel[(bs,)](
830	                    prefix_lens,
831	                    seq_lens,
832	                    last_loc,
833	                    self.free_virtual_ids,
834	                    out_indices,
835	                    next_power_of_2(bs),
836	                    self.page_size,
837	                )
838	
839	            # Bind the consumed virtual pages to fresh physical pages here. The
840	            # peer (swa side) binds the same pages via `alloc_with_virtual`.
841	            if new_virtual_pages is not None:
842	                phys_pages = self._alloc_bind_fast_or_slow(
843	                    new_virtual_pages, num_new_pages
844	                )
845	                if phys_pages is None:
846	                    return None  # defensive; pre-check should have prevented it
847	
848	            self.free_virtual_ids = self.free_virtual_ids[num_new_pages:]
849	            return out_indices  # virtual token ids
850	
851	    def alloc_decode(
852	        self,
853	        seq_lens: torch.Tensor,
854	        seq_lens_cpu: torch.Tensor,
855	        last_loc: torch.Tensor,
856	    ) -> Optional[torch.Tensor]:
857	        """Allocate one new token per request (decode), preserving the
858	        tail-page-reuse contract. Runs in virtual space; binds each consumed
859	        virtual page on THIS sub-allocator (else v2p stays -1 → CUDA OOB).
860	        """
861	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.alloc_decode"):
862	            assert (
863	                self.is_id_owner
864	            ), f"alloc_decode on a non-id-owner allocator ({self.sub_pool_name!r})"
865	            bs = len(seq_lens)
866	            # CPU-only count BEFORE the kernel, to snapshot the exact slice the
867	            # kernel will consume.
868	            num_new_pages = get_num_new_pages(
869	                seq_lens=seq_lens_cpu, page_size=self.page_size, decode=True
870	            )
871	            if num_new_pages > len(self.free_virtual_ids):
872	                return None
873	            # Lazy: physical-capacity pre-check; on shortfall flush PEER.
874	            need_tokens = num_new_pages * self.page_size
875	            if need_tokens > self.available_size():
876	                if not self._flush_peer_for_alloc(need_tokens):
877	                    return None
878	            if self.need_sort and bs > len(self.free_virtual_ids):
879	                self.merge_and_sort_free()
880	
881	            # Most decode steps reuse the prefix's tail page → num_new_pages == 0.
882	            if num_new_pages > 0:
883	                new_virtual_pages = self.free_virtual_ids[:num_new_pages].clone()
884	            else:
885	                new_virtual_pages = None
886	
887	            out_indices = torch.empty((bs,), dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device)
888	            with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.alloc_decode.kernel"):
889	                alloc_decode_kernel[(bs,)](
890	                    seq_lens,
891	                    last_loc,
892	                    self.free_virtual_ids,
893	                    out_indices,
894	                    next_power_of_2(bs),
895	                    self.page_size,
896	                )
897	
898	            if new_virtual_pages is not None:
899	                phys_pages = self._alloc_bind_fast_or_slow(
900	                    new_virtual_pages, num_new_pages
901	                )
902	                if phys_pages is None:
903	                    return None
904	
905	            self.free_virtual_ids = self.free_virtual_ids[num_new_pages:]
906	            return out_indices  # virtual token ids
907	
908	    # -- free with eager compaction --
909	
910	    def free(self, free_index: torch.Tensor) -> None:
911	        """Free virtual TOKEN ids: recover virtual PAGE ids, un-map v2p/p2v,
912	        (if id-owner) recycle the page ids, trigger eager compaction.
913	
914	        `free_index` is token-granular and need not be page-aligned. EAGER mode
915	        drops one `wait_stream(forward_stream)` barrier so v2p/p2v writes and the
916	        compaction move serialize with the in-flight forward. LAZY mode needs no
917	        barrier (a freed `v` has no live reader, so the scatters are
918	        disjoint-element from any forward read, atomic on Ampere+/Hopper) and
919	        defers compaction to `_flush`.
920	        """
921	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.free"):
922	            if free_index is None or free_index.numel() == 0:
923	                return
924	            if not self.is_not_in_free_group:
925	                self.free_group.append(free_index)
926	                return
927	            if self.lazy_compaction:
928	                self._free_lazy(free_index)
929	                return
930	            # --- EAGER path ---
931	            # Near-no-op in normal mode (sampling's CPU sync already drained
932	            # forward_stream); in overlap mode it serializes free+compaction with
933	            # the in-flight forward.
934	            if self.forward_stream is not None:
935	                with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.free.wait_stream"):
936	                    torch.cuda.current_stream().wait_stream(self.forward_stream)
937	            with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.free.v2p_lookup"):
938	                free_v_pages = torch.unique(
939	                    free_index.detach().to(torch.int64) // self.page_size
940	                )
941	                freed_p_pages = self.virtual_to_physical[free_v_pages]
942	            with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.free.sync_check"):
943	                # `.item()` forces a CPU/GPU sync — own trace region to measure it.
944	                if bool((freed_p_pages < 0).any().item()):
945	                    self._raise_stale_slot_assertion(
946	                        free_v=free_v_pages, freed_p=freed_p_pages
947	                    )
948	            self.virtual_to_physical[free_v_pages] = -1
949	            if self.is_id_owner:
950	                self.free_virtual_ids = torch.cat([self.free_virtual_ids, free_v_pages])
951	            self._compact_pending(freed_p_pages)
952	
953	    def _free_lazy(self, free_index: torch.Tensor) -> None:
954	        """Lazy free path: disjoint-element scatters + ONE `torch.cat` onto
955	        `_free_phys_pages`. No sort, no boundary absorb, no watermark mutation,
956	        no D2H sync. Boundary absorption is deferred to `_flush`.
957	
958	        ps==1 skips `torch.unique` (token == page and `free_index` is already
959	        unique per caller contract); ps>1 needs it to dedup same-page tokens.
960	        Callers must not double-free: a tombstone (-1) here would be cat'd onto
961	        the free list.
962	        """
963	        self._stats_n_free_lazy += 1
964	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc._free_lazy"):
965	            with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc._free_lazy.v2p_lookup"):
966	                free_v_pages_raw = free_index.detach().to(torch.int64)
967	                if self.page_size == 1:
968	                    free_v_pages = free_v_pages_raw
969	                else:
970	                    free_v_pages = torch.unique(free_v_pages_raw // self.page_size)
971	                freed_p_pages = self.virtual_to_physical[free_v_pages]
972	            # Disjoint-element scatters — no barrier (a freed v has no live reader;
973	            # per-element scatter writes are atomic).
974	            self.virtual_to_physical[free_v_pages] = -1
975	            self.physical_to_virtual[freed_p_pages] = -1
976	            if self.is_id_owner:
977	                self.free_virtual_ids = torch.cat([self.free_virtual_ids, free_v_pages])
978	            self._free_phys_pages = torch.cat([self._free_phys_pages, freed_p_pages])
979	            if _SORT_FREE_LIST_AFTER_MERGE:
980	                self._free_phys_pages, _ = torch.sort(self._free_phys_pages)
981	            self.live_page_count -= int(freed_p_pages.shape[0])
982	
983	    def _release_phys_pages_batch(self, pages: torch.Tensor) -> None:
984	        """Cat `pages` onto `_free_phys_pages` (+ optional sort). Called by `_flush`
985	        at END to merge event-fired compaction-srcs (`released_fired`) AFTER the
986	        trailing dst-slice, keeping `_free_phys_pages == holes_cpu` during the walk.
987	
988	        No watermark / `live_page_count` change — these are vacated src positions
989	        re-entering as PURE storage, not freshly-freed live pages.
990	        """
991	        if pages.numel() == 0:
992	            return
993	        self._stats_n_release_batch += 1
994	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc._release_phys_pages_batch"):
995	            self._free_phys_pages = torch.cat([self._free_phys_pages, pages])
996	            if _SORT_FREE_LIST_AFTER_MERGE:
997	                self._free_phys_pages, _ = torch.sort(self._free_phys_pages)
998	
999	    def _compact_pending(self, freed_physical_pages: torch.Tensor) -> None:
1000	        """Eager compaction over the freed PHYSICAL pages: move survivors from the
1001	        vacated band (K pages adjacent to the watermark) into the holes in the kept
1002	        band, advance the watermark, remap the tables. `src`/`dst` are disjoint by
1003	        construction, so the batched copy is order-independent. The caller's
1004	        `wait_stream` barrier already serialized us with the in-flight forward.
1005	        """
1006	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc._compact_pending"):
1007	            self._compact_pending_impl(freed_physical_pages)
1008	
1009	    def _compact_pending_impl(self, freed_physical_pages: torch.Tensor) -> None:
1010	        freed_set = set(int(x) for x in freed_physical_pages.tolist())
1011	        if not freed_set:
1012	            return
1013	        K = len(freed_set)
1014	        if self.grow_direction == "up":
1015	            # allocated == [min_page_index, old_wm); after the free == [min_page_index, new_wm)
1016	            old_wm = self.watermark_physical
1017	            new_wm = old_wm - K
1018	            assert new_wm >= self.min_page_index, (
1019	                f"_compact_pending({self.sub_pool_name!r}): freeing {K} pages "
1020	                f"would push the watermark below min_page_index "
1021	                f"({new_wm} < {self.min_page_index})"
1022	            )
1023	            assert all(self.min_page_index <= h < old_wm for h in freed_set), (
1024	                f"_compact_pending({self.sub_pool_name!r}): freed physical pages "
1025	                f"{sorted(freed_set)} not all within allocated range "
1026	                f"[{self.min_page_index}, {old_wm})"
1027	            )
1028	            # vacated band = [new_wm, old_wm); kept band = [min_page_index, new_wm)
1029	            src_list = [s for s in range(new_wm, old_wm) if s not in freed_set]
1030	            dst_list = sorted(h for h in freed_set if h < new_wm)
1031	            self.watermark_physical = new_wm
1032	            vacated_lo, vacated_hi = new_wm, old_wm
1033	        else:
1034	            # allocated == (old_wm, num_pages); after the free == (new_wm, num_pages)
1035	            old_wm = self.watermark_physical
1036	            new_wm = old_wm + K
1037	            assert new_wm <= self.num_pages - 1, (
1038	                f"_compact_pending({self.sub_pool_name!r}): freeing {K} pages "
1039	                f"would push the watermark above num_pages "
1040	                f"({new_wm} > {self.num_pages - 1})"
1041	            )
1042	            assert all(old_wm < h < self.num_pages for h in freed_set), (
1043	                f"_compact_pending({self.sub_pool_name!r}): freed physical pages "
1044	                f"{sorted(freed_set)} not all within allocated range "
1045	                f"({old_wm}, {self.num_pages})"
1046	            )
1047	            # vacated band = (old_wm, new_wm] = [old_wm+1, new_wm+1); kept band = (new_wm, num_pages)
1048	            src_list = [s for s in range(old_wm + 1, new_wm + 1) if s not in freed_set]
1049	            dst_list = sorted(h for h in freed_set if h > new_wm)
1050	            self.watermark_physical = new_wm
1051	            vacated_lo, vacated_hi = old_wm + 1, new_wm + 1
1052	
1053	        assert len(src_list) == len(dst_list), (
1054	            f"_compact_pending({self.sub_pool_name!r}): {len(src_list)} survivors vs "
1055	            f"{len(dst_list)} holes — corrupt allocator state"
1056	        )
1057	
1058	        if src_list:
1059	            src_pages = torch.tensor(src_list, dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device)
1060	            dst_pages = torch.tensor(dst_list, dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device)
1061	            v_moved = self.physical_to_virtual[
1062	                src_pages
1063	            ].clone()  # read before clearing
1064	
1065	            # Expand page ids to token ids for the token-granular move kernel.
1066	            if self.page_size == 1:
1067	                src_t, dst_t = src_pages, dst_pages
1068	            else:
1069	                offsets = torch.arange(
1070	                    self.page_size, dtype=torch.int64, device=self.device
1071	                )
1072	                src_t = (src_pages[:, None] * self.page_size + offsets).reshape(-1)
1073	                dst_t = (dst_pages[:, None] * self.page_size + offsets).reshape(-1)
1074	
1075	            # Un-translated copy: the public copy_from translates virtual ids,
1076	            # which we must NOT do here.
1077	            move_fn = getattr(self._kvcache, "move_kv_cache", None)
1078	            if move_fn is not None:
1079	                move_fn(dst_t, src_t)
1080	            else:
1081	                copy_phys = getattr(self._kvcache, "_copy_from_physical", None)
1082	                assert copy_phys is not None, (
1083	                    f"sub-pool {self.sub_pool_name!r} supports neither move_kv_cache "
1084	                    "nor _copy_from_physical"
1085	                )
1086	                copy_phys(src_t, dst_t)
1087	            # Clear the vacated band, then re-bind the relocated dst pages.
1088	            self.physical_to_virtual[vacated_lo:vacated_hi] = -1
1089	            self.virtual_to_physical[v_moved] = dst_pages
1090	            self.physical_to_virtual[dst_pages] = v_moved
1091	            self._inverse_history.append((src_pages, dst_pages, v_moved))
1092	        else:
1093	            self.physical_to_virtual[vacated_lo:vacated_hi] = -1
1094	
1095	    # -- lazy compaction primitives --
1096	
1097	    def set_latest_forward_done_event(self, event: Optional[torch.cuda.Event]) -> None:
1098	        """Stash the most-recent forward's `forward_done` event; `_pending_reuse`
1099	        uses it to gate src reuse on read-path settling. None = no in-flight forward.
1100	        """
1101	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.set_latest_forward_done_event"):
1102	            self._latest_forward_done_event = event
1103	
1104	    def set_inflight_forward(
1105	        self,
1106	        forward_done: torch.cuda.Event,
1107	        out_cache_loc_virtual: Optional[torch.Tensor],
1108	    ) -> None:
1109	        """Stash the just-launched forward's `forward_done` event + virtual
1110	        `out_cache_loc` for `_flush`'s write-race check.
1111	
1112	        No GPU work — only references; `_flush` materializes the write-set lazily
1113	        on `schedule_stream`, avoiding a launch-time sync. Pass
1114	        `out_cache_loc_virtual=None` when the forward doesn't write this pool
1115	        (e.g. Mamba state, written by mamba kernels not `set_kv_buffer`). No-op
1116	        in eager mode.
1117	        """
1118	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc.set_inflight_forward"):
1119	            if not self.lazy_compaction:
1120	                return
1121	            if out_cache_loc_virtual is None or out_cache_loc_virtual.numel() == 0:
1122	                # No write race on this pool — clear the slot so `_flush`
1123	                # short-circuits and the prior tensor reference can be GC'd.
1124	                self._inflight_forward = None
1125	                return
1126	            self._inflight_forward = (forward_done, out_cache_loc_virtual)
1127	
1128	    def _materialize_inflight_write_set(self) -> Optional[Set[int]]:
1129	        """Materialize the in-flight forward's write-set (physical PAGE ids it is
1130	        about to write), or `None` if no in-flight forward / already completed.
1131	        Called inside `_flush` on `schedule_stream`. Pays a bs-sized D2H sync, but
1132	        only once per call and only when a survivor needs classifying.
1133	        """
1134	        inflight = self._inflight_forward
1135	        if inflight is None:
1136	            return None
1137	        event, oclv = inflight
1138	        # Forward completed → no write race. Clear so later flushes in the same
1139	        # tick don't re-check the fired event.
1140	        if event.query():
1141	            self._inflight_forward = None
1142	            return None
1143	        # `oclv` is non-None here (set_inflight_forward clears the slot otherwise).
1144	        with record_function("MultiEndedAlloc._materialize_inflight_write_set"):
1145	            phys_tokens = self.translate_kv_loc(oclv)
1146	            if self.page_size > 1:
1147	                phys_pages = (phys_tokens // self.page_size).unique()
1148	            else:
1149	                phys_pages = phys_tokens
1150	            return set(phys_pages.tolist())  # .tolist() syncs schedule_stream
1151	
1152	    def _maybe_emit_stats(self) -> None:
1153	        """Env-gated periodic stats emit (at most once per interval) at `_flush` end.
1154	        Disabled unless `SGLANG_LOG_LAZY_COMPACTION_STATS=1`.
1155	        """
1156	        if not _LAZY_COMPACTION_STATS_ENABLED:
1157	            return
1158	        now = _time_mod.monotonic()
1159	        if now - self._stats_last_emit_ts < _LAZY_COMPACTION_STATS_INTERVAL_SEC:
1160	            return
1161	        self._stats_last_emit_ts = now
1162	        self._stats_n_emits += 1
1163	        cur_holes = int(self._free_phys_pages.shape[0])
1164	        cur_pending = len(self._pending_reuse_pages_cpu)
1165	        self._stats_peak_free_list_len = max(self._stats_peak_free_list_len, cur_holes)
1166	        self._stats_peak_pending_pages = max(
1167	            self._stats_peak_pending_pages, cur_pending
1168	        )
1169	        sort_tag = "ON" if _SORT_FREE_LIST_AFTER_MERGE else "OFF"
1170	        logger.info(
1171	            f"[lazy-stats sub={self.sub_pool_name!r}] "
1172	            f"free_lazy={self._stats_n_free_lazy} "
1173	            f"flush={self._stats_n_flush_calls} "
1174	            f"(work={self._stats_n_flush_did_work} "
1175	            f"moves={self._stats_n_flush_moves} "
1176	            f"abs={self._stats_n_pages_absorbed}) "
1177	            f"drain={self._stats_n_drain_did_work}/{self._stats_n_drain_calls} "
1178	            f"sort={sort_tag} "
1179	            f"peak_holes={self._stats_peak_free_list_len} "
1180	            f"peak_pending={self._stats_peak_pending_pages} "
1181	            f"cur_holes={cur_holes} cur_pending={cur_pending} "
1182	            f"live={self.live_page_count} wm={self.watermark_physical}"
1183	        )
1184	
1185	    def _emit_stats_final(self, reason: str = "exit") -> None:
1186	        """Force-emit final counters at shutdown (bypasses the interval gate).
1187	        Idempotent (signal handler + atexit may both fire); best-effort.
1188	        """
1189	        if not _LAZY_COMPACTION_STATS_ENABLED:
1190	            return
1191	        if self._stats_final_emitted:
1192	            return
1193	        try:
1194	            cur_holes = int(self._free_phys_pages.shape[0])
1195	            cur_pending = len(self._pending_reuse_pages_cpu)
1196	            self._stats_peak_free_list_len = max(
1197	                self._stats_peak_free_list_len, cur_holes
1198	            )
1199	            self._stats_peak_pending_pages = max(
1200	                self._stats_peak_pending_pages, cur_pending
1201	            )
1202	            sort_tag = "ON" if _SORT_FREE_LIST_AFTER_MERGE else "OFF"
1203	            self._stats_final_emitted = True
1204	            logger.info(
1205	                f"[lazy-stats FINAL sub={self.sub_pool_name!r} reason={reason}] "
1206	                f"free_lazy={self._stats_n_free_lazy} "
1207	                f"flush={self._stats_n_flush_calls} "
1208	                f"(work={self._stats_n_flush_did_work} "
1209	                f"moves={self._stats_n_flush_moves} "
1210	                f"abs={self._stats_n_pages_absorbed}) "
1211	                f"drain={self._stats_n_drain_did_work}/{self._stats_n_drain_calls} "
1212	                f"sort={sort_tag} "
1213	                f"peak_holes={self._stats_peak_free_list_len} "
1214	                f"peak_pending={self._stats_peak_pending_pages} "
1215	                f"cur_holes={cur_holes} cur_pending={cur_pending} "
1216	                f"live={self.live_page_count} wm={self.watermark_physical} "
1217	                f"n_emits={self._stats_n_emits}"
1218	            )
1219	        except Exception:
1220	            pass
